title: allow for user provided configuration to be included in containers.conf and registries.conf for podman machine
body:
** BUG REPORT or FEATURE**

/kind feature

**Description**

When creating a new podman machine (podman machine init) with defaults the contents of configuration files are hardcoded from pkg/machine/ignition.go , specifically lines [442](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/ignition.go#L442 ) and [303](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/ignition.go#L303)

This is a request to allow for user data to be provided (rather than a complete custom crafted ignition file) when initialising a podman machine. Perhaps have the Go script [ignition.go](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/ignition.go) to read contents from local files or templates.

See StackOverflow for real life issue description: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/72832064/podman-only-search-in-docker-io/74270998


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out @viecili!

Apologies for the delayed reply.

Users can change these settings by configuring the VMs once created.  I am a bit reluctant to special-case the config files, so I am looking to generalize the request a bit.

Would a `--mount` option work that would mount specified files/directories to a specified path?  Similar to `podman run --mount...`?  I think that `podman machine init --volume` mounts directories only.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

that could work @vrothberg !

for some of these files it may also be necessary to enable overriding/setting environment variables in the VM, e.g. 

`podman machine init -v $CONT_CONF_LOCAL:$CONT_CONF_TGT -e CONTAINERS_CONF=$CONT_CONF_TGT/containers.conf`

what do you think?

You should not use CONTAINERS_CONF to overwrite the config. This will cause podman to only use this single config file and thus will not use the correct settings that are set by `podman machine` in `/etc/containers.containers.conf` unless you copy all settings manually into your config.
You could drop the customer config into a containers.conf.d directory.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we suggested fixes and have not heard any further comment, I am closing.  Reopen if I am mistaken of continue the conversation here.
